title: Bridge Essay: Disrupted emotion regulation and spontaneous neural activity in panic disorder: a resting-state fMRI study.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Non-fabrication** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Non-fabrication — spontaneous thought, mind-wandering, creative insight, DMN.
**Phase context:** Spontaneous thought, mind-wandering, creative cognition.

**Disrupted emotion regulation and spontaneous neural activity in panic disorder: a resting-state fMRI study.** — Ther Adv Psychopharmacol (2024). DOI: 10.1177/20451253241298871. PMID: 39552918. BACKGROUND: Emotional dysregulation, particularly unconscious catastrophic cognitions, plays a pivotal role in the genesis of panic disorder (PD). However, no studies have yet applied the percentage of amplitude fluctuation (PerAF) metric in resting-state functional magnetic resonance imaging to examine spontaneous neural functioning and its relation to catastrophic cognitions in PD.
